page: The Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-800s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor. It features 16 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU allocation is shared, and the system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of an 800 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark results indicate a top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 10% of all tested servers. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores align with average mid-tier expectations. Memory performance is a notable strength, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%, supported by a read-only small-block memory bandwidth of 2293.31 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the server achieves strong text generation speeds of 293.29 tokens per second on small 135M parameter language models, while medium 7B model inference and database operations perform at average levels.

From a resource density perspective, this server offers a high-capacity memory configuration relative to its shared CPU allocation, making it a cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. However, the shared CPU allocation represents a tradeoff, as multi-core processing capabilities remain in the average tier. This hardware profile is well-suited for memory-optimized databases, caching layers like Redis, static web serving, and moderate-scale data processing tasks.
description: The Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-800s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC-Rome processor with a 2.0 GHz clock speed. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is provided by an 800 GB NVMe SSD. While its single-core CPU performance ranks in the top 10% in stress-ng benchmarks, its multi-core performance and database operations align with mid-tier averages. The server's memory subsystem is highly capable, scoring in the top 25% on PassMark memory tests. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and shared CPU allocation, this server is a cost-effective option for memory-intensive workloads, including caching layers, database hosting, and static web serving.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-800s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and an 800 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong memory performance for database and caching workloads.

tagline: An AMD-powered virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and fast NVMe storage for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory benchmark scores
